HER GRAVE. Kevuratah (her grave) and kivrah (her grave) mean the same.18Kever means a grave, kevurah a burial, and kevuratah her burial. Thus our verse should have read kivrah (her grave) and not kevuratah (her burial) since Jacob erected a monument over Rachel’s grave. I.E. points out that kevuratah can also have the same meaning as kivrah (Krinsky).
